text: The frequency of the \(n\)th harmonic in a closed pipe (one end closed) is:
explanation: Closed pipe: fundamental \( f_1 = v/4L \). Only odd harmonics: \( f_n = (2n-1)v/4L \). Open pipe: all harmonics present.
name: Closed Pipe Harmonics
subject: phy

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1) \) to line \( ax+by+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to line: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+c|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2}} \)
name: Point-Line Distance
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6 = 0 \), then \( \alpha\beta\gamma = \)
explanation: Product of roots of \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \) is \( -r = -(-6) = 6 \)
name: Product of Roots of Cubic
subject: maths

text: The term independent of \(x\) in \( \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^6 \) is:
name: Term Independent of x
subject: maths

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2,z_2) \) in 3D is:
explanation: 3D Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2+(z_2-z_1)^2} \)
name: 3D Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: The relationship between \( \Delta G^\circ \) and \( E^\circ_{cell} \) is:
explanation: \( \Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ_{cell} \). At equilibrium: \( \Delta G = 0 \), \( E = 0 \). Also \( \Delta G^\circ = -RT\ln K \).
name: ΔG° and E°cell
subject: che

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: The rectangular hyperbola has eccentricity:
explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
subject: maths

text: The real part of \( \frac{1}{1-\cos\theta + i\sin\theta} \) is:
explanation: After rationalizing and simplifying using half-angle identities, the real part equals \( \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Real Part Extraction
subject: maths

text: Torque \(\vec{\tau} = \vec{r} \times \vec{F}\). Its magnitude is:
explanation: Torque magnitude: \( \tau = rF\sin\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{r}\) and \(\vec{F}\). Maximum when \(\theta = 90°\).
name: Torque Magnitude
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: The area of parallelogram with adjacent sides \( \vec{a} \) and \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Area of parallelogram \( = |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \). Area of triangle \( = \frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Area Using Cross Product
subject: maths

text: The number of atoms per unit cell in an FCC structure is:
explanation: FCC: 8 corners × (1/8) + 6 face centres × (1/2) = 1 + 3 = 4 atoms per unit cell.
name: Atoms in FCC Unit Cell
subject: che

text: For parametric equations \( x=f(t), y=g(t) \), \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Parametric differentiation: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt} = \frac{g'(t)}{f'(t)} \)
name: Parametric Differentiation
subject: maths

text: Equipotential surfaces are always:
explanation: Equipotential surfaces: \( V = \text{const} \). Always perpendicular to \(\vec{E}\). For point charge: concentric spheres. For uniform field: parallel planes.
name: Equipotential Surfaces
subject: phy

text: The number of terms in the expansion of \( (a+b)^n \) is:
explanation: The expansion of \( (a+b)^n \) has \( n+1 \) terms, from \( T_1 \) to \( T_{n+1} \).
name: Number of Terms
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Hyperbola (transverse axis along x): \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \). Eccentricity \(e > 1\).
name: Hyperbola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The radical axis of \( S_1=0 \) and \( S_2=0 \) is:
explanation: Radical axis: \( S_1 - S_2 = 0 \), which eliminates \(x^2\) and \(y^2\) terms.
name: Radical Axis Equation
subject: maths

text: The value of \( 1 \cdot 1! + 2 \cdot 2! + 3 \cdot 3! + \cdots + n \cdot n! \) is:
name: Factorial Sum
subject: maths

text: A body of mass 2 kg moving at 3 m/s has kinetic energy:
name: KE Calculation
subject: phy

text: \( \sin 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \sin 3\theta = 3\sin\theta - 4\sin^3\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: A material that shows large plastic deformation before fracture is called:
explanation: Ductile materials (e.g., copper, steel): large plastic deformation before fracture. Brittle materials (glass, cast iron): fracture with little or no plastic deformation.
name: Ductile vs Brittle
subject: phy

text: Gauss's law for magnetism states:
explanation: Gauss's law for magnetism: \( \oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{A} = 0 \). Magnetic field lines form closed loops (no magnetic monopoles exist).
name: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
subject: phy

text: The condition for constructive interference between two waves is path difference:
explanation: Constructive interference: path difference \( = 0, \lambda, 2\lambda, ... = n\lambda \). Destructive: \( = \lambda/2, 3\lambda/2, ... = (2n+1)\lambda/2 \).
name: Constructive Interference Condition
subject: phy

text: Ethers are cleaved by concentrated HI or HBr (not HCl) because:
options: I⁻ and Br⁻ are better nucleophiles than Cl⁻
Ethers are stable to HCl only
explanation: Ether cleavage: R–O–R + HI → R–OH + R–I. I⁻ is a better nucleophile than Cl⁻. HI > HBr > HCl in ether cleavage.
name: Ether Cleavage
subject: che

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: The work done by a torque \(\tau\) through angular displacement \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Rotational work: \( W = \tau\theta \). Analogous to linear work \( W = Fs \).
name: Work by Torque
subject: phy

text: Chloroform (CHCl₃) when oxidised in air slowly gives:
explanation: CHCl₃ oxidises in air to form phosgene (COCl₂), which is highly toxic. That's why CHCl₃ is stored in dark bottles with a little ethanol.
name: Oxidation of Chloroform
subject: che

text: In the Arrhenius plot of \(\ln k\) vs \(\frac{1}{T}\), the slope is:
explanation: \( \ln k = \ln A - \frac{E_a}{RT} \). Slope of \(\ln k\) vs \(1/T\) graph \( = -E_a/R \).
name: Arrhenius Plot Slope
subject: che

text: The eddy currents in a conductor placed in a changing magnetic field:
options: Produce heat (energy dissipation) and oppose the change (useful in braking)
explanation: Eddy currents: closed loop currents in bulk conductor in changing \(B\). Cause: heating (loss in motors/transformers). Uses: induction furnace, magnetic braking, speedometers.
name: Eddy Currents
subject: phy

text: Maxwell added the displacement current term to Ampere's law. It is:
explanation: Displacement current: \( I_d = \epsilon_0 \frac{d\Phi_E}{dt} \). This resolved the inconsistency in Ampere's law for capacitor circuits and predicted EM waves.
name: Displacement Current
subject: phy

text: The term 'bandwidth' in communication refers to:
options: Range of frequencies occupied by the signal
explanation: Bandwidth: range of frequencies in a signal or channel. Higher bandwidth → more information per second (Shannon's theorem: capacity \( = B\log_2(1+SNR) \)).
name: Bandwidth Definition
subject: phy

text: The highest oxidation state shown by Mn is:
explanation: Mn can show +7 in KMnO₄ (potassium permanganate). Mn: [Ar]3d⁵4s² → can lose all 7 valence electrons.
name: Highest Oxidation State of Mn
subject: che

text: A block of mass 5 kg is on a frictionless surface. A force of 20 N is applied. Its acceleration is:
name: Newton's 2nd Law Calculation
subject: phy

text: Optical isomerism (enantiomers) is possible in:
options: Octahedral complexes with three bidentate ligands [M(AA)₃]
explanation: Optical isomers (non-superimposable mirror images) occur in [M(AA)₃] type octahedral complexes (e.g., [Co(en)₃]³⁺). Also [M(AA)₂BC]²⁺.
name: Optical Isomerism in Complexes
subject: che

text: Lucas test distinguishes between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols using:
explanation: Lucas test: 3° alcohol → immediate turbidity (SN1, stable 3° carbocation). 2° → turbidity in 5 min. 1° → no turbidity at room temperature.
name: Lucas Test
subject: che

text: The lensmaker's equation is:
explanation: Lensmaker's equation: \( \frac{1}{f} = (n-1)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right) \). \(R_1\), \(R_2\) = radii of curvature of two surfaces.
name: Lensmaker's Equation
subject: phy

text: In a binomial distribution \( B(n, p) \), the mean is:
explanation: Mean of Binomial Distribution \( = np \), Variance \( = npq \) where \( q = 1-p \).
name: Binomial Mean
subject: maths

text: For \( f(x) = x\sin\frac{1}{x} \), \( \lim_{x\to 0}f(x) = \)
explanation: \( |x\sin\frac{1}{x}| \leq |x| \to 0 \). By squeeze theorem, \( \lim_{x\to 0}x\sin\frac{1}{x} = 0 \).
name: Oscillating Limit
subject: maths

text: The troposphere extends from Earth's surface to approximately:
explanation: Troposphere: 0–12 km (approx). Contains 75% of atmosphere's mass. Weather occurs here. Temperature decreases with altitude.
name: Troposphere Height
subject: che

text: The floor function \( \lfloor 3.7 \rfloor = \)
explanation: \( \lfloor x \rfloor \) = greatest integer \( \leq x \). So \( \lfloor 3.7 \rfloor = 3 \)
name: Floor Function
subject: maths

text: A reaction is spontaneous at all temperatures when:
explanation: \( \Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S \). When \(\Delta H < 0\) and \(\Delta S > 0\): \(\Delta G < 0\) always (at all T).
name: Spontaneous at All T
subject: che

text: The Mean Value Theorem guarantees \( \exists\, c \in (a,b) \) such that:
explanation: Lagrange's MVT: \( f'(c) = \frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a} \) for some \(c \in (a,b)\).
name: Mean Value Theorem
subject: maths

text: Aldehydes and ketones are distinguished by Tollens' reagent (ammoniacal AgNO₃). Aldehydes give:
explanation: Tollens' test: RCHO + 2[Ag(NH₃)₂]⁺ + 2OH⁻ → RCOOH + 2Ag↓ + 4NH₃. Silver mirror formed. Ketones give no reaction.
name: Tollens' Test
subject: che

text: The diameter of the \(n\)th dark ring in Newton's rings is:
explanation: Newton's rings dark ring: \( D_n = 2\sqrt{n\lambda R} \), where \(R\) = radius of curvature of lens. \(D_n^2 \propto n \) (linear with \(n\)).
name: Newton's Rings Diameter
subject: phy

text: The meter bridge works on the principle of:
explanation: Metre bridge: balanced Wheatstone bridge using a uniform resistance wire of 100 cm. \( R/X = l/(100-l) \) gives unknown resistance X.
name: Metre Bridge
subject: phy

text: In triangle \(ABC\), \( \tan\frac{A}{2} = \)
explanation: \( \tan\frac{A}{2} = \frac{r}{s-a} = \sqrt{\frac{(s-b)(s-c)}{s(s-a)}} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Half-angle Tangent
subject: maths

text: de Broglie's explanation of Bohr's quantisation: the condition \( mvr = n\hbar \) is equivalent to:
options: The orbit must be an integer multiple of radius
The circumference must contain integer number of de Broglie wavelengths: \( 2\pi r = n\lambda \)
explanation: de Broglie: \( \lambda = h/mv \). For standing wave in orbit: \( 2\pi r = n\lambda = nh/mv \Rightarrow mvr = nh/2\pi \). Beautifully explains Bohr's ad hoc condition.
name: de Broglie and Bohr's Quantisation
subject: phy

explanation: Special formula: \( \int e^x[f(x)+f'(x)]dx = e^xf(x)+C \). Very useful shortcut.
name: eˣ[f+f'] Integration
subject: maths

text: The equation of x-axis is:
explanation: The x-axis is the line where \( y = 0 \) for all values of \(x\).
name: Equation of Axes
subject: maths

text: Lithium differs from other Group 1 metals due to its diagonal relationship with:
explanation: Li resembles Mg (diagonal relationship): both form nitrides, hydroxides/carbonates are less soluble, LiCl/MgCl₂ are covalent.
name: Li Diagonal Relationship
subject: che

text: Carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) is given by:
options: Primary amines (1°) to give foul-smelling isocyanide
explanation: Carbylamine test: 1° amine + CHCl₃ + KOH → isocyanide (R–NC), very unpleasant smell. Positive only for primary amines. Test for 1° amines.
name: Carbylamine Test
subject: che

explanation: \( \sin^{-1}x + \cos^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} \) for all \( x \in [-1,1] \). Important identity.
name: sin⁻¹ + cos⁻¹ Identity
subject: maths

text: The electric flux through a surface is maximum when the surface is:
explanation: \( \Phi = EA\cos\theta \). Maximum when \( \theta = 0 \) (field perpendicular to surface, i.e., area vector parallel to \(\vec{E}\)).
name: Maximum Electric Flux
subject: phy

text: \( \sin 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \). Also \( = \frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} \).
name: Double Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: If \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B \) is called:
explanation: By definition, if \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B = A^{-1} \), the inverse of \(A\).
name: Matrix Inverse Definition
subject: maths

text: Using Binomial theorem, the approximate value of \( (1.01)^5 \) up to 3 decimal places is:
name: Binomial Approximation
subject: maths

text: The cosine rule gives:
explanation: Cosine Rule: \( a^2 = b^2 + c^2 - 2bc\cos A \)
name: Cosine Rule
subject: maths

text: \( ^nC_r = \binom{n}{r} \) satisfies \( ^nC_r = \)
explanation: Symmetry property: \( \binom{n}{r} = \binom{n}{n-r} \)
name: Symmetry of Combinations
subject: maths

text: Work done by a force \(\vec{F}\) through displacement \(\vec{s}\) is:
explanation: Work \( W = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{s} = Fs\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{F}\) and \(\vec{s}\).
name: Work Done by Force
subject: phy

text: If the length of a simple pendulum is quadrupled, its time period:
explanation: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{l/g} \propto \sqrt{l} \). If \( l \to 4l \): \( T \to 2\pi\sqrt{4l/g} = 2T \). Period doubles.
name: Effect of Length on Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: The incenter of triangle with vertices \( A, B, C \) and opposite sides \( a, b, c \) is:
explanation: Incenter \( = \left(\frac{ax_A+bx_B+cx_C}{a+b+c}, \frac{ay_A+by_B+cy_C}{a+b+c}\right) \) (weighted by opposite side lengths).
name: Incenter Formula
subject: maths

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: For an ideal gas, the adiabatic process follows:
explanation: Adiabatic process: \( PV^\gamma = \text{const} \), where \( \gamma = C_p/C_v > 1 \). More steep than isothermal curve on P-V diagram.
name: Adiabatic Process Equation
subject: phy

text: The entropy change \(\Delta S\) for a reversible process at temperature \(T\) is:
explanation: Entropy change: \( dS = dQ_{rev}/T \), or \( \Delta S = Q/T \) for isothermal reversible process. For reversible adiabatic: \( \Delta S = 0 \).
name: Entropy Change
subject: phy

text: The half-life \(T_{1/2}\) of a radioactive substance is related to decay constant \(\lambda\) by:
explanation: Half-life: \( T_{1/2} = \ln 2/\lambda = 0.693/\lambda \). After \(n\) half-lives: \( N = N_0/2^n \).
name: Half-Life Formula
subject: phy

text: Malus's law states that when plane-polarised light of intensity \(I_0\) passes through analyser at angle \(\theta\):
explanation: Malus's law: \( I = I_0\cos^2\theta \). At \(\theta=0\): maximum transmission. At \(\theta=90°\): no transmission (extinction).
name: Malus's Law
subject: phy

text: The velocity-time graph for uniform acceleration is:
options: A straight line with positive slope
explanation: For uniform acceleration: \(v = u + at\) is linear in \(t\). v-t graph is a straight line with slope \(= a\).
name: v-t Graph for Uniform Acceleration
subject: phy

text: Area of triangle \(ABC\) using two sides and included angle is:
explanation: Area \( = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C = \frac{1}{2}bc\sin A = \frac{1}{2}ca\sin B \)
name: Area of Triangle
subject: maths

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference
subject: maths

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{f(x)}{(x-a)^2(x-b)} \) is:
explanation: For repeated linear factor \((x-a)^2\), decompose as \( \frac{A}{x-a} + \frac{B}{(x-a)^2} \), plus \( \frac{C}{x-b} \).
name: Repeated Linear Factors
subject: maths

text: \( \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \) can also be written as:
explanation: By Euler's formula: \( e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \)
name: Euler's Formula
subject: maths

text: The range of \( f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 5 \) is:
explanation: Minimum at \( x=2 \): \( f(2) = 4-8+5 = 1 \). Since \(a>0\), range is \([1, \infty)\)
name: Range of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity \(20\) m/s. Maximum height reached (\(g=10\) m/s²) is:
explanation: \( v^2 = u^2 - 2gH \Rightarrow 0 = 400 - 2(10)H \Rightarrow H = 20\,\text{m} \).
name: Max Height - Vertical Throw
subject: phy

text: Electronegativity difference between atoms determines:
explanation: Higher electronegativity difference → more ionic character. \(\Delta\chi > 1.7\): predominantly ionic; \(< 1.7\): covalent.
name: Electronegativity and Bond Type
subject: che

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = \)
explanation: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\sin\theta \). Area of parallelogram = \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Magnitude of Cross Product
subject: maths

text: The modern periodic law states that properties of elements are periodic functions of their:
explanation: Moseley's Modern Periodic Law: Properties are periodic functions of atomic number (not mass as Mendeleev stated).
name: Modern Periodic Law
subject: che

text: The angle made by the tangent to \( y=x^2 \) at \( (1,1) \) with x-axis is:
name: Angle of Tangent
subject: maths

text: For matrices \( A \) and \( B \), \( (AB)^T = \)
explanation: Reversal law for transpose: \( (AB)^T = B^T A^T \)
name: Transpose of Product
subject: maths

text: The potential gradient (\(dV/dr\)) at the surface of a charged conductor is related to surface charge density \(\sigma\) by:
explanation: Just outside conductor: \( E = \sigma/\epsilon_0 \) and \( E = -dV/dr \Rightarrow dV/dr = -\sigma/\epsilon_0 \). (Negative: potential decreases away from +ve charge)
name: Potential Gradient at Conductor Surface
subject: phy

text: The wattless current (reactive current) in an AC circuit flows through:
options: Inductance and capacitance (pure L or pure C)
explanation: Wattless (reactive) current: component that does no real work (90° out of phase with voltage). Flows through L and C. Power factor = 0.
name: Wattless Current
subject: phy

text: Cannizzaro reaction (disproportionation) is given by:
explanation: Cannizzaro: aldehydes lacking α-H (HCHO, PhCHO, CCl₃CHO) undergo disproportionation in conc. NaOH → one mole oxidised to acid, one reduced to alcohol.
name: Cannizzaro Reaction
subject: che

text: Resonance stabilisation is maximum when:
explanation: Resonance stabilisation is greatest when all contributing structures have equal (or similar) energies. Equal structures → more stabilisation.
name: Resonance Stabilisation
subject: che

text: Mutual inductance \(M\) between two coils is defined by:
options: \( \varepsilon_2 = M\frac{dI_1}{dt} \) (EMF in coil 2 due to changing current in coil 1)
explanation: Mutual inductance: \( \varepsilon_2 = -M\frac{dI_1}{dt} \). \( M = N_2\Phi_{21}/I_1 \). Unit: Henry. \( M \leq \sqrt{L_1L_2} \) (equality when fully coupled).
name: Mutual Inductance
subject: phy

explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{a^2+x^2}dx = \frac{1}{a}\tan^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Standard Form Integral
subject: maths

text: If \( |A| = 5 \) and \( A \) is \( 3 \times 3 \), then \( |\text{adj}(A)| = \)
name: Adjoint Determinant
subject: maths

text: According to Aufbau principle, the order of orbital filling is:
explanation: Aufbau (n+l rule): 4s (n+l=4) fills before 3d (n+l=5). Order: 1s, 2s, 2p, 3s, 3p, 4s, 3d, 4p...
name: Aufbau Principle
subject: che

text: Reverse osmosis is used for water purification because:
options: Applying pressure > osmotic pressure forces solvent from solution to pure water side
explanation: Reverse osmosis: applying pressure greater than osmotic pressure forces solvent (water) through semi-permeable membrane, leaving solute behind.
name: Reverse Osmosis
subject: che

text: The industrial preparation of hydrogen by steam reforming of methane gives:
explanation: Steam reforming: \( \text{CH}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\text{Ni, 1270K}} \text{CO} + 3\text{H}_2 \) (synthesis gas/water gas). Most H₂ is produced this way.
name: Steam Reforming
subject: che

text: Walli's formula: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^m x\cos^n x\,dx \) involves the pattern of multiplying down by \(2\) at the end when both \(m\) and \(n\) are:
explanation: When both \(m\) and \(n\) are even, Walli's formula requires an additional factor of \(\frac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Walli's Formula
subject: maths

text: The area between curve \( y=f(x) \) and x-axis from \(a\) to \(b\) is:
explanation: Area \( = \int_a^b |f(x)|\,dx \). Use \( |f(x)| \) to handle regions below x-axis.
name: Area Under Curve
subject: maths

text: Anti-Markovnikov addition (peroxide effect) occurs in addition of HBr to alkenes when:
explanation: In presence of peroxides, HBr adds via free radical mechanism (anti-Markovnikov). Br goes to carbon with more H (opposite to ionic). Only HBr shows this.
name: Peroxide Effect
subject: che

text: The intercept form of a straight line is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{b} = 1 \), where \(a\) = x-intercept, \(b\) = y-intercept.
name: Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The denominator \( x^3 - x = x(x-1)(x+1) \). The partial fraction of \( \frac{1}{x^3-x} \) has how many terms?
explanation: Three distinct linear factors \(x, (x-1), (x+1)\) give three partial fraction terms.
name: Number of Terms
subject: maths

text: The equation whose roots are squares of roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Roots of original: 1, 2. Squared: 1, 4. New equation: \( (x-1)(x-4) = x^2 - 5x + 4 = 0 \)
name: Squared Roots Equation
subject: maths

text: In a p-n junction, the depletion region is formed by:
options: Diffusion of majority carriers across junction, leaving ionised atoms
explanation: Depletion region: electrons from n-side diffuse to p-side, holes from p-side to n-side. Ionised donors (n-side) and acceptors (p-side) remain → built-in potential barrier.
name: Depletion Region Formation
subject: phy

text: Area enclosed between curves \( y=f(x) \) and \( y=g(x) \) where \(f(x)\geq g(x)\) is:
explanation: Area between two curves: \( \int_a^b [f(x)-g(x)]\,dx \) when \(f \geq g\) on \([a,b]\).
name: Area Between Curves
subject: maths

text: The expression \( x^2 + x + 1 \) is positive for:
explanation: Discriminant \( = 1 - 4 = -3 < 0 \) and \( a = 1 > 0 \), so expression is positive for all real \( x \).
name: Sign of Quadratic Expression
subject: maths

text: The dimensional formula of Planck's constant \(h\) (where \(E = h\nu\)) is:
name: Dimensions of Planck's Constant
subject: phy

text: Clemmensen reduction converts ketone to:
explanation: Clemmensen reduction: R–CO–R' + Zn(Hg)/conc. HCl → R–CH₂–R'. Reduces C=O to CH₂ (removes oxygen). For acid-tolerant substrates.
name: Clemmensen Reduction
subject: che

text: The hybridisation of carbon in ethyne (\( \text{C}_2\text{H}_2 \)) is:
explanation: In \(\text{C}_2\text{H}_2\) (acetylene): triple bond between C atoms → \(sp\) hybridisation, linear shape (180°).
name: Hybridisation in Ethyne
subject: che

text: Pascal's law states that pressure applied to an enclosed fluid:
options: Acts only at the point of application
Is transmitted equally in all directions throughout the fluid
explanation: Pascal's Law: pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in all directions (hydraulic press principle).
name: Pascal's Law
subject: phy

text: From 6 men and 4 women, a committee of 3 men and 2 women can be formed in:
name: Committee Formation
subject: maths

text: Markovnikov's rule states that in addition of HX to an unsymmetrical alkene:
options: H goes to the carbon with fewer H atoms
X goes to the carbon with fewer H atoms
H goes to the carbon with more H atoms
The reaction gives equal amounts of both products
explanation: Markovnikov's rule: H adds to the C with more hydrogens (H-rich carbon). This forms the more stable carbocation intermediate.
name: Markovnikov's Rule
subject: che

text: Sandmeyer reaction converts diazonium salt to aryl halide using:
explanation: Sandmeyer: ArN₂⁺ + CuCl → ArCl + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuBr → ArBr + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuCN → ArCN + N₂. Replaces N₂ with X or CN.
name: Sandmeyer Reaction
subject: che

text: Bayes' theorem states: \( P(A_i|B) = \)
explanation: Bayes' theorem: \( P(A_i|B) = \frac{P(A_i)P(B|A_i)}{\sum_j P(A_j)P(B|A_j)} \)
name: Bayes' Theorem
subject: maths

text: The potentiometer is used to measure EMF because it draws:
explanation: Potentiometer: null method — at balance, no current flows through galvanometer. Gives accurate EMF without drawing current from source.
name: Potentiometer Principle
subject: phy

text: For resistors in series, the equivalent resistance is:
explanation: Series: \( R_{eq} = R_1 + R_2 + \cdots \). Current is same through all. Parallel: \( 1/R_{eq} = 1/R_1 + 1/R_2 \).
name: Resistors in Series
subject: phy

text: The general solution of \( \sin\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = 0 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \).
name: General Solution of sinθ=0
subject: maths

text: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution on aryl halides is facilitated by:
explanation: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr): electron-withdrawing groups (–NO₂) ortho/para to leaving group stabilise the Meisenheimer complex intermediate.
name: Nucleophilic Aromatic Substitution
subject: che

text: The angle between two planes is equal to the angle between their:
explanation: Dihedral angle between planes = angle between their normal vectors.
name: Angle Between Planes
subject: maths

text: The work function \(\phi_0\) of a metal is the minimum energy required to:
options: Remove an electron from the metal surface
explanation: Work function \(\phi_0\): minimum energy to liberate an electron from the metal surface. Varies with metal: Cs (lowest ≈2eV) to Pt (highest ≈5.7eV).
name: Work Function
subject: phy

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: Interhalogen compounds like ClF₃ are formed because:
options: Larger halogens can expand their octet using d orbitals
explanation: Interhalogens: formed between two different halogens. Larger halogens (Cl, Br, I) use d orbitals to accommodate more F atoms. E.g., IF₇.
name: Interhalogen Compounds
subject: che

text: The variation of \(g\) with depth \(d\) inside Earth is:
explanation: Inside Earth: \( g' = g\left(1 - \frac{d}{R}\right) \). At centre (d=R): g'=0. Decreases linearly going inside.
name: g Inside Earth
subject: phy

text: The electric field \(\vec{E}\) at a point is defined as:
options: Force on any charge placed there
Force per unit positive test charge: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \)
explanation: Electric field: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \) (force per unit positive test charge). Units: N/C = V/m.
name: Definition of Electric Field
subject: phy

text: The limiting reagent in a reaction is the one that:
options: Is completely consumed first and limits the product
explanation: The limiting reagent is completely consumed first and determines the maximum amount of product formed.
name: Limiting Reagent
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) = x^3 \), then \( f \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = (-x)^3 = -x^3 = -f(x) \). So \(f\) is an odd function.
name: Odd Function Check
subject: maths

text: The length of tangent from external point \((x_1,y_1)\) to circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Length of tangent \( = \sqrt{S_1} = \sqrt{x_1^2+y_1^2+2gx_1+2fy_1+c} \)
name: Length of Tangent
subject: maths

text: The modulus of \( z = \frac{1+2i}{3-4i} \) is:
name: Modulus of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: Which element among 3d series has maximum number of unpaired electrons?
explanation: Mn: [Ar]3d⁵4s². 3d⁵ has 5 unpaired electrons (Hund's rule). Maximum for 3d series is 5 (at Mn, d⁵).
name: Maximum Unpaired Electrons
subject: che

text: The empirical formula represents:
options: The actual number of atoms in a molecule
The simplest whole number ratio of atoms
explanation: Empirical formula gives the simplest whole number ratio of atoms. Molecular formula = n × (empirical formula).
name: Empirical Formula
subject: che

text: The radius of circular motion of a charged particle in a magnetic field is:
explanation: Circular motion in \(B\): centripetal force = magnetic force: \( \frac{mv^2}{r} = qvB \Rightarrow r = \frac{mv}{qB} \). Used in mass spectrometer.
name: Radius of Circular Motion in B
subject: phy

text: The argument of \( z = -1 - i \) is:
explanation: \( z = -1-i \) lies in 3rd quadrant. \( \arg(z) = -(\pi - \frac{\pi}{4}) = -\frac{3\pi}{4} \)
name: Argument of Complex Number
subject: maths

text: For a conical pendulum, the tension in the string provides:
options: Both vertical support and centripetal force (horizontal component provides centripetal)
explanation: Conical pendulum: \( T\cos\theta = mg \) (vertical), \( T\sin\theta = \frac{mv^2}{r} \) (centripetal horizontal).
name: Conical Pendulum
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{x^3}{x-1} \) requires partial fractions after:
explanation: Since degree of numerator (3) > degree of denominator (1), perform long division first to get a proper fraction.
name: Improper Fraction - Long Division
subject: maths

text: Freundlich adsorption isotherm is given by:
explanation: Freundlich isotherm: \( \frac{x}{m} = kP^{1/n} \) or \( \log\frac{x}{m} = \log k + \frac{1}{n}\log P \).
name: Freundlich Isotherm
subject: che

text: The DE representing all parabolas with axis parallel to y-axis is of order:
explanation: General parabola \( y=ax^2+bx+c \) has 3 arbitrary constants. Differentiating 3 times gives order 3.
name: Order from Parabola Family
subject: maths

text: Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions states:
options: Limiting molar conductivity = sum of ionic conductivities of cation and anion
Ions migrate only at infinite dilution
explanation: Kohlrausch's Law: \( \Lambda^\infty_m = \nu_+ \lambda^\infty_+ + \nu_- \lambda^\infty_- \). Each ion contributes independently.
name: Kohlrausch's Law
subject: che

text: A catalyst in a chemical equilibrium:
options: Increases rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally
Only increases rate of forward reaction
explanation: A catalyst increases rates of both forward and reverse reactions equally. It does not change K or shift equilibrium position.
name: Catalyst and Equilibrium
subject: che

text: The midpoint of segment joining \( (x_1,y_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2) \) is:
explanation: Midpoint Formula: \( M = \left(\frac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \frac{y_1+y_2}{2}\right) \)
name: Midpoint Formula
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \sin^2\theta = \sin^2\alpha \Rightarrow \cos 2\theta = \cos 2\alpha \Rightarrow 2\theta = 2n\pi \pm 2\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \pm \alpha \)
name: sin²θ = sin²α
subject: maths

text: EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) is a _____ ligand:
explanation: EDTA⁴⁻ is a hexadentate (polydentate) ligand: 2 N donors + 4 O donors = 6 donor atoms. Forms very stable chelates with metal ions.
name: EDTA as Hexadentate Ligand
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to a} \frac{x^n - a^n}{x - a} = \)
explanation: Standard algebraic limit: \( \lim_{x\to a}\frac{x^n-a^n}{x-a} = na^{n-1} \)
name: Algebraic Limit
subject: maths

text: A diatomic gas (like N₂, O₂) at moderate temperature has degrees of freedom:
explanation: Diatomic: 3 translational + 2 rotational = 5 degrees of freedom. \( C_v = \frac{5}{2}R \), \( \gamma = 7/5 = 1.4 \).
name: Degrees of Freedom - Diatomic
subject: phy

text: For circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \), the radius is:
explanation: Radius \( r = \sqrt{g^2+f^2-c} \). Requires \( g^2+f^2-c > 0 \) for real circle.
name: Radius from General Form
subject: maths

text: If the sum of two roots of \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \) is zero, then:
explanation: Let roots be \( \alpha, -\alpha, \beta \). Then \( \alpha+(-\alpha)+\beta = -p \Rightarrow \beta=-p \). Since \(\beta\) is root: use Vieta's relations to get \(pq = r\).
name: Condition on Roots Sum
subject: maths

text: Resonance structures of \( \text{O}_3 \) show that the actual O–O bond order is:
explanation: Ozone has two resonance structures (one O=O and one O–O). Average bond order = (1+2)/2 = 1.5.
name: Resonance in Ozone
subject: che

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The domain of \( \tanh x \) is:
explanation: \( \tanh x \) is defined for all real numbers since \( \cosh x > 0 \) always.
name: Domain of tanh
subject: maths

text: The number of arbitrary constants in a general solution equals:
explanation: General solution of an nth-order DE contains exactly \(n\) arbitrary constants.
name: Constants in General Solution
subject: maths

text: The knee voltage (cut-in voltage) for a silicon p-n junction diode is approximately:
explanation: Si diode: knee voltage ≈ 0.7 V. Ge diode: ≈ 0.3 V. Below knee voltage, current is negligible. Si diodes are more common (higher temperature stability).
name: Knee Voltage of Si Diode
subject: phy

explanation: Logarithm product rule: \( \log(mn) = \log m + \log n \)
name: Log Product Rule
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \)
explanation: \( \sin\frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{1}{2} \), and \( \frac{\pi}{6} \in \left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \). So \( \sin^{-1}\frac{1}{2} = \frac{\pi}{6} \).
name: sin⁻¹(1/2)
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(e^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(e^x) = e^x \). The exponential function is its own derivative.
name: Derivative of eˣ
subject: maths

text: The number of ways \(n\) people can sit around a circular table is:
explanation: For circular arrangements, one position is fixed: \( (n-1)! \) ways.
name: Circular Permutation
subject: maths

text: For proving \( P(n): 2^n > n \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), the base case is verified at \( n = \)
explanation: At \( n=1 \): \( 2^1 = 2 > 1 \). Base case holds.
name: Base Case Verification
subject: maths

text: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is:
name: Zero Order Half-life
subject: che

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: A function \( f: A \to B \) is bijective if it is:
explanation: Bijective means one-to-one (injective) AND onto (surjective).
name: Bijective Function Definition
subject: maths

text: Two coins are tossed. \( P(\text{exactly one head}) = \)
explanation: Sample space: \{HH, HT, TH, TT\}. Exactly one head: \{HT, TH\}. \( P = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Coin Toss Probability
subject: maths

text: The average kinetic energy of gas molecules is:
explanation: Average KE per molecule \( = \frac{3}{2}kT \). Per mole: \( \frac{3}{2}RT \). (k = Boltzmann constant)
name: Average KE of Gas
subject: che

text: Adding an inert gas at constant volume to an equilibrium mixture:
explanation: At constant volume, adding inert gas doesn't change partial pressures of reactants/products → no shift in equilibrium.
name: Inert Gas at Constant Volume
subject: che

text: Soaps are sodium or potassium salts of:
explanation: Soap: sodium/potassium salts of long-chain fatty acids (C₁₂–C₁₈). Made by saponification: fat + NaOH → soap + glycerol.
name: Soap Formation
subject: che

text: The shielding effect is maximum for which electrons?
explanation: s-electrons have maximum penetration to nucleus and thus maximum shielding effect. Shielding: s > p > d > f.
name: Shielding Effect
subject: che
